10 Practical Tips to Improve React Native Performance
Performance is one of the biggest factors that determines whether users love or uninstall your app.
A feature-rich application means very little if it feels slow, consumes too much memory, or stutters while scrolling.
The good news is that you don’t need complex optimizations to build a fast React Native application. Small improvements applied consistently can make a huge difference.
Here are 10 practical techniques I use while developing React Native CLI applications.
1. Use FlatList Instead of ScrollView
Rendering hundreds of items inside a ScrollView increases memory usage because everything is rendered at once.
FlatList virtualizes items and only renders what's visible on the screen.
Useful props include:

• keyExtractor

• initialNumToRender

• windowSize

• maxToRenderPerBatch

• getItemLayout
2. Avoid Unnecessary Re-renders
Every unnecessary render consumes CPU resources.
Use:

• React.memo

• useMemo

• useCallback
But only when profiling shows that optimization is actually needed.
3. Optimize Images
Large images slow down application startup and increase memory usage.
Best practices:

• Compress assets

• Use correct dimensions

• Prefer WebP where supported

• Lazy load images when possible
4. Lazy Load Heavy Screens
Not every screen should be loaded immediately.
React.lazy and dynamic imports help reduce the initial bundle size and improve startup performance.
Load features only when users actually need them.
5. Keep State Local
Global state should only contain shared application data.
Keeping temporary UI state local reduces unnecessary renders across the application.
Choose the right solution:

• Context API

• Zustand

• Redux Toolkit
depending on project complexity.
6. Use Native Driver for Animations
Animations running on the JavaScript thread can become laggy.
Whenever supported, enable the Native Driver in the Animated API to achieve smoother animations.
7. Remove Console Logs Before Production
Leaving debugging logs inside production builds creates unnecessary overhead.
Always remove console statements before releasing your application.
8. Reduce Bundle Size
A smaller bundle loads faster.
Regularly:

• Remove unused dependencies

• Import only what you need

• Review package sizes

• Split features when appropriate

9. Always Clean Up useEffect
Memory leaks often happen because developers forget cleanup functions.
Always remove:

• Event listeners

• Timers

• Subscriptions
This keeps your application stable over long sessions.
10. Measure Performance Regularly
Optimization without measurement is just guessing.
Use tools like:

• React DevTools

• Flipper

• Android Studio Profiler

• Xcode Instruments
Measure first, optimize second.
Final Thoughts
Performance isn’t a feature you add at the end of development.
It’s a habit that starts with writing clean architecture, choosing efficient components, and making thoughtful engineering decisions every day.
Most production performance issues aren’t caused by one big mistake — they’re the result of many small inefficiencies.
Focus on continuous improvement, and your React Native applications will become faster, more stable, and easier to maintain.
